summary: Full regression for Aria v8.0 — CookPilot Integration Phase 1. Created /app/backend/tests/test_cookpilot.py with 14 pytest cases — ALL 14 PASSED. Frontend smoke verified via Playwright (admin flow complete). CookPilot module shape, permissions, service-router decisions, admin UI, sidebar submenu and error handling all working correctly when CookPilot service is not configured (as expected in this preview env).

minor.issue: service_router.check_service_available('cookpilot') calls cookpilot.is_available() which does a 3s httpx GET when URL is set but unreachable. In preview this correctly returns False, but if admin sets a broken URL, EVERY chat request would incur a 3s blocking roundtrip just to decide service availability. Consider caching the health result for 30-60s.
Uses bson.ObjectId(user_id) without try/except — a malformed user_id crashes with InvalidId (500 not 400). Minor robustness gap.

passed_tests: TestNonDevServicesContainsCookpilot (server.py NON_DEV_SERVICES set literally includes cookpilot)
TestStickyBreakCookpilotMonkeyPatch (monkey-patched router→['cookpilot'] + seeded sticky forgepilot → forgepilot.query_forgepilot NOT called, sentinel msg absent)

admin_tab: cookpilot-url-input / cookpilot-secret-input / cookpilot-save / cookpilot-test all present
cookpilot-test button click → cookpilot-test-result renders 'Schritt url: CookPilot URL nicht gesetzt' (graceful, no 500, no UI crash)
sidebar: data-testid=nav-cookpilot visible for admin (all perms → all 6 items)
All 6 submenu testids present: recipes, meal-plan, shopping, pantry, chat, tablet
iframe_error_handling: /cookpilot/recipes route renders cookpilot-embed-error block ('URL oder Shared Secret fehlt') instead of crashing
not_tested_via_playwright: Per-user perm filter for luzia sidebar (only 'Rezepte' visible after admin saves visible+recipes_view only) — luzia login step timed out in the 2nd browser session in this run (email input not found after cookies.clear). Permission logic itself IS validated at backend level by TestCookpilotPerms (persistence) + TestCookpilotStatus::test_luzia_status_shape (response shape). Frontend uses those same fields and filters the submenu list in LcarsLayout.jsx:147-152, so behavior follows directly from the verified backend.

action_items: (Optional) Cache service_router.check_service_available('cookpilot') result for 30-60s so a broken CookPilot URL doesn't add 3s latency to every /api/chat call.
(Optional) Wrap bson.ObjectId(user_id) in PUT /api/cookpilot/admin/users/{id}/perms with try/except → 400 on malformed ids.
Previous minor items from iteration_13 still valid: (a) persist placeholder assistant msg when OpenAI fails, (b) HA-keyword fast-path should also write routed_to=['homeassistant'].
critical_code_review_comments: cookpilot._proxy correctly returns 503 (not 500) when URL is empty — verified.
cookpilot.get_user_perms correctly falls back to DEFAULT_PERMS per key — verified for luzia.
cookpilot.PERM_KEYS (12 keys) and DEFAULT_PERMS are in sync — consider adding a unit-level assertion that set(PERM_KEYS) == set(DEFAULT_PERMS.keys()) so future additions don't drift.
cookpilot.init() stashes get_current_user + require_admin as module globals. If init is somehow called twice or in a different order, handler references become stale. Minor — works fine as-is.

LcarsLayout.jsx filters submenu items via p.recipes_view / p.shopping_view etc. If Admin saves perms with empty dict {}, get_user_perms on backend falls back to defaults (correct), but the status endpoint returns those defaults — so UI shows defaults. This matches backend behavior and is correct; just worth documenting that 'no perms saved yet' = DEFAULT_PERMS.

Admin.jsx stores cpSecret in plain state and sends it in a generic /api/settings PUT. Backend masks it on reads (server.py:1059) but ensure POST/PUT doesn't accidentally log the full secret to audit-log. Not verified in this run.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_cookpilot.py (NEW — 14 pytest cases covering all Phase-1 CookPilot acceptance criteria)

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (admin flow + sidebar + iframe error handling verified; luzia sidebar perm-filter not end-to-end browser-tested but backend verified)

seed_data_creation: autouse fixture wipes db.settings keys cookpilot_url + cookpilot_shared_secret BEFORE each test (matches preview-env assumption of 'CookPilot unconfigured'). TestCookpilotPerms resets luzia's cookpilot_perms to broad defaults after the negative-perm test. TestStickyBreakCookpilotMonkeyPatch seeds + cleans a single TEST_sticky_cp_* chat session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CookPilot is NOT reachable in preview (by design). The 'unconfigured → 503, not 500' contract is the critical gate and is green. For end-to-end proxy testing when CookPilot IS reachable, point db.settings.cookpilot_url at a stub server returning 200 on /api/aria/health and a valid JWT on POST /api/aria/sso — then cookpilot._proxy will succeed. In this env cookpilot.is_available() returns False, so service_router never includes cookpilot in the live available_services list from /api/chat; the sticky-break test therefore uses monkey-patching on service_router.route_message + cookpilot.is_available to exercise the guarded branch deterministically. Previous sticky_routing tests remain green EXCEPT TestVersion::test_version_is_7_3 which now fails (expected — version bumped to 8.0). Remove that test or update to 8.0 if it needs to stay.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — this was a feature-add regression pass, not a bug fix. All 14 acceptance criteria from the review_request map cleanly to passing tests. The only risk area flagged is the 3-second health probe on every /api/chat when cookpilot URL is set but unreachable — see action_items.
